steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the angle sum property
steps.content: The sum of all interior angles in any triangle equals $180^\circ$. We know $\angle K = 34^\circ$ and $\angle L = 34^\circ$, so set up an equation to find $\angle J$.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum formula and solve
steps.content:
The sum of angles in triangle $JKL$:
$$\angle J + \angle K + \angle L = 180^\circ$$
Substitute the known angles:
$$\angle J + 34^\circ + 34^\circ = 180^\circ$$
Combine like terms:
$$\angle J + 68^\circ = 180^\circ$$
Subtract $68^\circ$ from both sides:
$$\angle J = 180^\circ - 68^\circ = 112^\circ$$
The measure of $\angle J$ is 112, so the answer is 112.
